title: STANDARD FOR CERTAIN CANNED VEGETABLES CXS 297-2009

sections.section: STANDARD FOR CERTAIN CANNED VEGETABLES CXS 297-2009
sections.summary: Title and amendment history of the standard for certain canned vegetables (CXS 297‑2009): adopted 2009 and amended in 2011, 2015, 2022 and 2023.
Notes 2022 amendments made to Section 8.3 (labelling of non‑retail containers) following decisions at the Forty‑fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
Notes 2023 amendments made to Section 9 (Methods of analysis and sampling) following decisions at the Forty‑sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

Supersedes previous standards and applies to specified canned vegetables offered for direct consumption (including catering or repackaging) but not intended for further processing, and excludes vegetables that are lacto‑fermented, pickled or preserved in vinegar.

Describes canned vegetables generally and introduces product definition and style provisions: products prepared from sound fresh (or frozen) vegetables, appropriately washed/prepared and heat‑processed, packed in suitable liquid or vacuum media and hermetically sealed for shelf‑stability, with allowance for other distinctive styles that meet the standard and are properly labeled.

Defines canned vegetables as products made from sound fresh (or frozen) vegetables prepared appropriately, packed in a suitable liquid packing medium or vacuum packed (packing medium not exceeding 20% of net weight) and heat‑processed before or after hermetic sealing to ensure stability at ambient temperatures.

Permits other styles of the product provided they are sufficiently distinctive, meet all relevant standard requirements (including limits on defects and drained weight) and are adequately described on the label to avoid misleading consumers.

Allows any other presentation of the product provided it is sufficiently distinctive from prescribed styles, meets all relevant standard requirements (including limits on defects, drained weight and requirements applicable to the most similar style), and is adequately described on the label to avoid consumer confusion or misleading.

Defines essential composition and quality factors: composition (vegetables and appropriate liquid packing media, with other ingredients as per annexes and specified packing media options), and quality requirements including normal sensory characteristics, defect limits set in annexes, the definition of a 'defective' container, and lot acceptance criteria (including sampling plans with an AQL of 6.5).
Heading only for Section 3.1 on composition; no substantive content in this subsection text.
Specifies basic ingredients as the vegetables defined in Section 2 and an appropriate liquid packing medium for the product.
States that other permitted ingredients shall be those provided for in the relevant provisions of the corresponding annexes.
Heading only for Section 3.1.3 on packing media; no substantive content in this subsection text.
Specifies basic packing media ingredients as water and, if necessary, salt.

Allows packing media to contain ingredients (subject to Section 8 labelling) including, but not limited to, sugars or sweeteners (e.g., honey), aromatic plants/spices/extracts and seasonings, vinegar, regular or concentrated fruit juice, oil, and tomato puree.
Heading only for Section 3.2 on quality criteria; no substantive content in this subsection text.
Requires canned vegetables to have normal colour, flavour and odour characteristic of the vegetable type and packing medium used and to possess the texture characteristic of the product.
States that canned vegetables should be substantially free from defects and that certain common defects must not exceed the limitations specified in the corresponding annexes.
Defines a 'defective' as any container failing one or more applicable quality requirements in Section 3.2, excluding requirements that are based on sample averages.

Specifies that a lot meets the quality requirements when non-average requirements have a number of 'defectives' not exceeding the acceptance number (c) of the sampling plan at an AQL of 6.5, and when requirements based on sample averages are complied with.

Permits only those classes and specific food additives listed in this section and the corresponding annexes to be used in foods under the standard, and only for the functions and within the limits specified.

sections.section: 4.1 Acidity regulators, colours, colour retention agents and calcium salts of firming agents used in accordance with Table 3 of the General standard for food additives (CXS 192-1995) 1  are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.
sections.summary: Specifies that acidity regulators, colours, colour retention agents and calcium salts of firming agents used in accordance with Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.
Lists permitted colour additives with maximum levels: INS 102 (Tartrazine) 100 mg/kg; INS 133 (Brilliant Blue FCF) 20 mg/kg; INS 143 (Fast Green FCF) 200 mg/kg; INS 150d (Caramel IV) 50,000 mg/kg.

Lists colour retention agents and limits: INS 385 (calcium disodium EDTA) 365 mg/kg singly or in combination, INS 386 (disodium EDTA), and INS 512 (stannous chloride) 25 mg/kg calculated as tin; stannous chloride should not be added to foods in uncoated tin cans.

Requires products to comply with the maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for low and acidified low-acid canned foods (CXC 23-1979) and other relevant Codex texts, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Heading only for Section 7 on weights and measures; no substantive content in this subsection text.
Only a heading for Section 7.1 (Fill of container); no substantive provisions included in this excerpt.

Requires the sealed container (product plus packing medium) to occupy at least 90% of the container's water capacity (volume of distilled water at 20°C), minus any necessary headspace per good manufacturing practice, with the provision not applying to vacuum-packaged vegetables.
States that any container failing to meet the minimum fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 shall be classified as a 'defective'.
A lot meets the minimum-fill requirement when the number of 'defective' containers (per Section 7.1.2) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan using an AQL of 6.5.

Introduces minimum drained weight requirements stating the drained weight must be not less than percentages specified in the corresponding annexes, calculated on the basis of the container's water capacity (distilled water at 20°C).

sections.section: 7.1.4.1 The drained weight of the product
sections.summary: Specifies that the drained weight of the product must be at least the percentages indicated in the relevant annexes, calculated relative to the weight of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

Requires compliance with minimum drained weight when the average drained weight of all examined containers is not less than the minimum required, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

States that products covered by this standard must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with additional specific provisions also applying.

Specifies naming and labelling obligations: product names must match those in the annexes, size may be declared with the name, the packing medium must be indicated (and 'vacuum packaged' stated where applicable), additional wording must prevent consumer confusion for 'other styles', and any added ingredient that alters flavour must be declared as 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured'.
Requires labelling of non‑retail containers to comply with the General standard for the labelling of non‑retail containers of foods (CXS 346-2021).
Only a heading for Section 9 (Methods of analysis and sampling); no substantive provisions included in this excerpt.
Mandates that methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard be those contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) for checking compliance.
Only a heading for Section 9.2 (Sampling); no substantive sampling procedures are provided in this excerpt.

Explains selection of inspection level: Inspection Level I is for normal sampling, while Inspection Level II is for disputes, Codex referee purposes, enforcement, or when a more precise lot estimate is needed.

Provides Sampling Plan 2 (Inspection Level II, AQL = 6.5) as tables specifying sample sizes (n) and acceptance numbers (c) by lot-size ranges for three net‑weight categories (≤1 kg; >1 kg to ≤4.5 kg; >4.5 kg).

sections.section: 1  FAO and WHO. 1995. General standard for food additives. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 192-1995. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.
sections.summary: Bibliographic reference: FAO and WHO (1995), General Standard for Food Additives, Codex Alimentarius Standard No. CXS 192-1995.

Annex specific to asparagus adding requirements to the general canned‑vegetable provisions, covering product definition, styles and sizing, colour categories, quality criteria and defect tolerances, minimum drained weights by style, and asparagus‑specific labelling rules.
Only a heading for '1 DESCRIPTION' within the asparagus annex; no substantive text in this excerpt.
Defines 'asparagus' as the product prepared from the tender edible portions of peeled or unpeeled stems of varieties complying with the characteristics of Asparagus officinalis L.
Only a heading for '1.2 Styles' (asparagus presentation types and sizing) in the annex; detailed style provisions are not included in this snippet.

sections.section: 1.2.1 Asparagus comes in the following shapes and sizes:
sections.summary: Defines asparagus presentation types by length: whole spears 12–18 cm, short spears 7–12 cm, tips 3–7 cm, cut sections 2–7 cm; for 'cut asparagus with tips' tips must be ≥15% of drained weight, while 'cut asparagus without tips' may contain occasional tips.

sections.section: 1.2.2 Asparagus are canned as follows in terms of their colour:
sections.summary: Classifies canned asparagus by colour: 'white' (white/cream/yellowish, with ≤20% of spears allowed green‑tinged tips), 'white with violet or green tips' (permits violet/green tips or adjoining regions but ≤25% of units may show these colours over >50% of their length), 'green' (green units, with ≤20% of units permitted to have white/cream lower parts over 20–50% of length in accordance with retail‑sale legislation), and 'mixed' (combinations of the listed colours).

Optional sizing: size corresponds to maximum diameter measured perpendicular to the spear axis and defines size categories (Small, Medium, Large, Very large, Colossal) with specified diameter ranges for white peeled, white unpeeled, and green asparagus.
Section heading for essential composition and quality factors; includes a note that maximum length should not exceed 9.5 cm in accordance with the legislation of the country of retail sale.
Heading only: introduces the quality criteria section (no substantive requirements in this item).

Length uniformity: 'reasonably uniform' means ≥75% of units must be within ±1 cm of the most frequent length and ≥90% within ±2 cm; diameter/size uniformity: when sold by individual size the sample must meet specified diameters and no more than 25% of units may be of adjacent sizes, with containers exceeding this 25% tolerance considered defective for sizing.

Defines defect types and maximum allowances: crushed pieces (product should be reasonably free), extraneous material (practically free), unpeeled zones in peeled asparagus 10% max, hollow/fibrous 10% max, deformed 10% max, damaged 15% max; cumulative maximum defect allowances by presentation: whole/short/tips 15% number, cut with tips 20%, cut without tips 25%.
Heading only: introduces weights and measures (no substantive requirements in this item).
Specifies minimum drained weight percentages by style: white peeled (whole/short) 59%, white unpeeled 57%, green asparagus 50%, other presentations 58%.

Labelling requirement: asparagus style declarations must include colour as defined in Section 1.2, and for white asparagus the words 'not peeled' and/or 'not sized' must be declared in accordance with the legislation of the country of retail sale.
